title: Insertion in an array - try it yourself!

content.problemStatement:
Let us see if you learnt insertion of an element at a given position.

TaskYou are given an array in the IDE. 

You are also given an element and an index.

Insert the element at the given index and output the new array to the console. 

Check the sample output below for further clarity.
constraints: Array elements and the inserted element are 32‑bit signed integers
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains the array elements separated by single spaces (the array length N satisfies 1 ≤ N ≤ 1000). The second line contains two integers: index (0 ≤ index ≤ N) and element, the value to be inserted at the given index.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the updated array after insertion. Elements are separated by a single space and no extra spaces appear at the beginning or end of the line.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Three test cases are processed.
1) Insert 7 at index 5 → 4 5 3 10 12 7 18.
2) Insert 10 at index 0 → 10 1 2 3.
3) Insert 100 at index 2 → 10 20 100 30.
Single element array [5]; inserting 9 at index 0 yields [9,5].
Insert 99 at the end of a 15‑element array (index equals N).

solution.approach: For each test case we read the whole line of the array, split it into integers and store them in a dynamic container (e.g., vector or list). The given index tells where the new element must appear. Inserting at position i can be done by shifting all elements from i to the end one position to the right (O(N)) and then writing the new value at i. All standard libraries provide an insert operation that internally performs this shift. After insertion we output the whole container separated by spaces. The algorithm is linear in the size of the array for each test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the original length of the array. Overall O(T * N_max) with N_max ≤ 1000.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) additional space for storing the array of each test case.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0].strip())
    idx = 1
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(t):
        # skip empty lines before array line
        while idx < len(data) and data[idx].strip() == '':
            idx += 1
        arr = list(map(int, data[idx].split()))
        idx += 1
        # skip empty lines before index line
        while idx < len(data) and data[idx].strip() == '':
            idx += 1
        index, element = map(int, data[idx].split())
        idx += 1
        arr.insert(index, element)
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, arr)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
